page: Hetzner Cloud cpx12 is an entry-level virtual server instance featuring a shared AMD EPYC Genoa processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 1 virtual CPU consisting of 1 physical core and 1 thread, running at a base speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor is paired with 2.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 1.95 GB per core. The instance includes 40 GB of local SSD storage, while graphics acceleration is absent with zero GPUs. The underlying virtualization is managed via QEMU.

In terms of performance, the shared AMD EPYC Genoa CPU delivers strong single-core performance in stress-ng benchmarks, placing in the top 25%. However, due to its single-core configuration, multi-core performance and PassMark CPU scores fall into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. Memory performance is mixed; while large-block write bandwidth is average, read-only bandwidth for both small and large blocks is relatively weak, scoring in the bottom 25%. Single-threaded operations, such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, perform at average levels.

The instance exhibits clear resource constraints for complex workloads. PassMark database operations and static web serving throughput are positioned in the bottom 10% of benchmarks. Similarly, Redis SET operations reach 2.79 million operations per second, which is in the bottom 25%. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the server processes prompts at 290.66 tokens per second and generates text at 107.47 tokens per second using a small 135M model.

Given its shared CPU allocation and modest 2.0 GB memory capacity, the cpx12 is highly cost-efficient for low-demand environments. It is best suited for lightweight utility workloads, basic web serving, development testing, and simple background tasks rather than resource-intensive database or high-throughput production applications.
description: Hetzner Cloud cpx12 is an entry-level virtual server configured with a single shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 2.0 GB of system memory and 40 GB of local SSD storage, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng, though multi-core and overall PassMark CPU scores remain in the bottom 10% to 25% due to the single-core limitation. Memory write bandwidth and single-threaded compression tasks perform at average levels, while database operations and static web serving throughput are weak. This shared-core instance offers high qualitative cost efficiency for budget-conscious deployments. It is best suited for lightweight workloads, including basic web serving, small-scale development environments, and simple background tasks.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx12 is a budget-friendly virtual server featuring 1 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 40 GB of SSD storage, ideal for lightweight utility workloads and development environments.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx12 is an entry-level virtual server with 1 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU, 2.0 GB RAM, and 40 GB SSD storage for basic workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, shared-core virtual server with solid single-core performance and local SSD storage for lightweight utility workloads.
bullet_points: 1 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPU on x86_64 architecture
2.0 GB of system memory and 40 GB of local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Mixed memory bandwidth with average large-block write speeds
Best suited for lightweight web serving and development tasks
